briefing: Sessions on May 19 came in at 37 -- up 85% over the 28-day median of 20 and up 61% versus the same day last year. That's a genuinely strong day, and the driver is clear: direct traffic jumped from a baseline of roughly 9.5 to 26 sessions, accounting for nearly the entire lift. New users doubled the 28-day median (28 vs. 14), pageviews hit 142 against a median of 74, and average session duration climbed to 143 seconds -- people who arrived stayed and read. This has the shape of a referral or share event rather than organic search growth, since GSC data is showing zeros across the board today (likely a reporting lag, not a real signal drop). On the efficacy side, the 30-day rollup shows 8 pages ranking in the top 3 and 8 in the top 10, with 49 GA4 sessions attributed to recent launches -- that's a reasonable answer to Todd's "is it working?" question, though GSC impressions attributed remain at zero pending data catch-up. Recent commits are all internal tooling and pulse files, nothing content-facing. Today: check whether Camy or anyone on the team posted or shared something Monday that could explain the direct spike -- a newsletter send, a social post, or a staff link would account for this pattern cleanly.
